description: Canonical restaurant operations ontology. Each metric has a stable id, human label, the synonyms/aliases used across POS exports, the formula (if derived), the valid sane range used for sanity-checking uploads, and the unit/format. This is the durable, accreting asset: every new POS quirk and customer correction lands here, not in code.

location.label: Location
location.description: A single physical restaurant unit within the operator's group.
location.notes: Multi-location operators are the core user. Location is the primary slicing dimension for almost every comparison.

date.label: Business Date
date.description: The operating/business date a record belongs to. NOT the same as the transaction timestamp — a sale at 1am may belong to the prior business date depending on the day-part close.
date.notes: Toast exports 'Business Date'; Square uses 'Date'; Clover uses 'Date' or a full timestamp that must be truncated to a calendar day.

daypart.label: Daypart
daypart.description: Service period bucket — breakfast, lunch, dinner, late night.

menu_item.label: Menu Item
menu_item.description: An individually sold item on the menu. The unit of product-mix (PMIX) analysis.

menu_category.label: Menu Category
menu_category.description: Grouping of menu items — Apps, Entrees, Beverages, Alcohol, Desserts.

net_sales.label: Net Sales
net_sales.description: Revenue from food and beverage sales AFTER discounts/comps and BEFORE tax. The denominator for nearly every cost percentage. Almost all restaurant KPIs are expressed as a % of net sales, not gross.
net_sales.notes: If both gross and net are present, OpticAI uses net for all percentage denominators and says so. Confusing gross for net silently overstates margins — a classic amateur mistake.

gross_sales.label: Gross Sales
gross_sales.description: Revenue BEFORE discounts and comps. Higher than net sales. Should not be used as the denominator for cost percentages.

direct_labor.label: Direct (Operational) Labor Cost
direct_labor.description: The unburdened raw cost of scheduled shifts: hourly wages, shift pay, and salaried floor-management pay tied to running the floor. This is the daily scheduling-efficiency number. Distinct from fully-burdened labor (see labor_burden). See calc_rules labor_basis.
direct_labor.notes: If the operator's labor_basis is 'fully_burdened' but only this direct figure is present, gross up via labor_burden_multiplier and label the result an estimate.

labor_burden.label: Labor Burden
labor_burden.description: Overhead costs of putting employees on the schedule, on top of direct wages: payroll taxes (FICA, Medicare, FUTA), benefits (health, 401k match), workers' comp, PTO, and uniforms. Adds roughly 15-25% to base wages.

foh_labor.label: Front of House Labor
foh_labor.description: Labor cost for servers, hosts, and bartenders. Lower base operational wage % of sales because FOH relies heavily on tips.

boh_labor.label: Back of House (Heart of House) Labor
boh_labor.description: Labor cost for chefs, line cooks, and dishwashers. Typically a HIGHER labor % of sales than FOH.

labor_pct.label: Labor %
labor_pct.description: Labor cost as a percentage of net sales. The single most-watched controllable cost line. Resolve to the operator's declared basis (direct vs. fully-burdened) and always state which.
labor_pct.notes: Benchmarks here are direct/operational labor. Fully-burdened runs ~4-6 points higher. If a direct labor_pct column exists, prefer it only if it agrees with computed value within ~1.5 points; otherwise flag — usually a different sales_basis or labor definition.

food_cost.label: Food Cost (COGS)
food_cost.description: STRICTLY the edible ingredients (proteins, produce, dairy, dry goods, spices). NOT the same as total COGS — Food Cost excludes beverage and paper/packaging. See calc_rules food_cost_definition. QSR/fast-casual may include packaging (set food_cost_definition=includes_paper).
food_cost.notes: If a column is labeled 'COGS' it may be TOTAL COGS (food+beverage+paper), not food cost alone. Confirm before treating it as food cost.

total_cogs.label: Total COGS
total_cogs.description: Sum of Food Cost + Beverage Cost + Paper/Packaging Cost. The full cost of goods sold. Distinct from Food Cost alone.

beverage_cost.label: Beverage Cost
beverage_cost.description: Cost of beverages, tracked SEPARATELY from food. Commonly segmented into soda/water (N/A), beer, wine, and liquor. Alcohol is ALWAYS beverage, never food cost.
beverage_cost.notes: Pour costs differ by type: liquor ~18-20%, beer ~24-28%, wine ~30-40%, soda <10%. Segment where possible.

beverage_sales.label: Beverage Sales
beverage_sales.description: Revenue from beverages — the correct denominator for beverage/pour cost %, not total sales.

paper_packaging.label: Paper & Packaging Cost
paper_packaging.description: Direct disposables that leave the building with food: to-go boxes, delivery bags, napkins, straws, utensils. Belongs in COGS as a separate line, NOT in Food Cost. Indirect supplies (restroom towels, kitchen trash bags, guest checks) are Operating Expenses, not COGS.
paper_packaging.notes: Target ~3-4% of net sales for limited-service, ~1-2% for full-service.

food_cost_pct.label: Food Cost %
food_cost_pct.description: Food cost (edible only) as a percentage of net sales. Distinct from total COGS %.

beverage_cost_pct.label: Beverage (Pour) Cost %
beverage_cost_pct.description: Beverage cost as a percentage of BEVERAGE sales (not total sales).

total_cogs_pct.label: Total COGS %
total_cogs_pct.description: Food + beverage + paper/packaging as a percentage of net sales.

prime_cost_pct.label: Prime Cost %
prime_cost_pct.description: Labor % + COGS % combined. The headline controllable-cost number. Some operators use labor + food cost only; others use labor + total COGS. Confirm which. Rule of thumb: keep under 60-65% of net sales for full service.
prime_cost_pct.formula: labor_pct + total_cogs_pct (or food_cost_pct per operator definition)
prime_cost_pct.notes: Prime cost above ~70% of net sales is a serious profitability warning in full-service. This is the number most operators check first.

check_count.label: Check Count
check_count.description: Number of guest checks/tickets closed. NOT the same as guest count (covers) — one check can cover multiple guests.
check_count.notes: Square's 'Transactions' usually means checks, not covers. This is a frequent ambiguity — confirm with user when the column is just 'transactions' or 'count'.

guest_count.label: Guest Count (Covers)
guest_count.description: Number of individual guests served (covers). Used for per-person spend and capacity analysis.
guest_count.notes: Square and Clover often do NOT track covers natively. If absent, per-guest metrics fall back to per-check.

average_check.label: Average Check
average_check.description: Net sales divided by number of checks. The per-ticket spend.
average_check.notes: PPA (per-person average) uses guest_count instead of check_count. If only checks are available, label the metric 'average check' not 'per-person average' to stay honest.

comps.label: Comps & Discounts
comps.description: Dollar value of complimentary items and discounts. High comps can mask service problems or theft.

comps_pct.label: Comps %
comps_pct.description: Comps and discounts as a percentage of gross or net sales.
comps_pct.notes: Comps above ~5% of sales warrant investigation — could be service recovery, manager abuse, or theft.

overtime_hours.label: Overtime Hours
overtime_hours.description: Hours paid at the overtime premium. A primary driver of labor cost overruns.

labor_hours.label: Labor Hours
labor_hours.description: Total hours worked across all staff for the period.

sales_per_labor_hour.label: Sales per Labor Hour (SPLH)
sales_per_labor_hour.description: Net sales divided by labor hours. A productivity measure independent of wage rates.

quantity_sold.label: Quantity Sold
quantity_sold.description: Units of a menu item sold. The basis of PMIX analysis.

item_revenue.label: Item Revenue
item_revenue.description: Revenue attributable to a single menu item — used in PMIX to rank menu performance.

food_cost_per_item.label: Item Food Cost
food_cost_per_item.description: The plate cost / recipe cost of a single menu item. Combined with price to compute item margin.

item_margin.label: Item Contribution Margin
item_margin.description: Price minus plate cost — the gross profit contributed by each unit sold. Drives menu engineering (stars/plowhorses/puzzles/dogs).

waste.label: Waste / Spoilage
waste.description: Quantity or dollar value of product wasted, spoiled, or thrown out.

inventory_variance.label: Inventory Variance
inventory_variance.description: Difference between theoretical (recipe-based) usage and actual usage. High variance signals waste, over-portioning, or theft.
inventory_variance.notes: Variance beyond +/- a few percent on a high-cost item (proteins especially) is a top diagnostic flag.

guest_satisfaction.label: Guest Satisfaction Score
guest_satisfaction.description: Aggregate guest rating — could be a star rating, NPS, or survey score depending on source.
guest_satisfaction.notes: Scale varies (1-5 stars, 0-10 NPS, 0-100). Detect scale from value range before interpreting.
